summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete. 32/32 NEW tests pass (CORS tightening, password complexity on 3 endpoints, faculty seeding for all 6 offices, bulk-process approve/reject + registrar gating, cursor pagination on audit-logs, authorization). Iteration 1 regression suite also re-run: 38/38 still passing. Total 70/70 backend tests green.

minor.issue: Kubernetes/Cloudflare ingress in front of the app injects 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' and 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true' regardless of what the FastAPI app returns. The application-level CORS allowlist at /app/backend/server.py is correct (verified by hitting localhost:8001 directly: evil.com gets no ACAO; localhost:3000 gets echoed back). However, browsers talking to the public preview URL will see the permissive ingress headers, which means the tightened CORS is effectively NOT enforced over the public domain. This is an infra concern, not an app-code bug, but worth flagging for production deployment where the ingress should be reconfigured to either (a) not add CORS headers and let the app handle them, or (b) match the same allowlist.

Returns dev_code:null when SENDGRID_API_KEY is set. Since the SendGrid sender is currently unverified, real users may never receive emails; tests work around this by reading the code from MongoDB. Recommend adding a delivery-status check to surface failures.
When cursor is provided, response omits total_count which is fine for cursor pagination, but consider adding it as optional metadata for UI page-size estimation.

action_items: OPTIONAL/INFRA: The kubernetes ingress is overriding the app's CORS headers with 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' on the public preview URL. The backend code is correct, but the ingress nullifies the tightening for browser-facing requests. Coordinate with platform/infra to align ingress CORS with app CORS, OR document explicitly that CORS is enforced only at the ingress layer and remove duplicate logic from the app.
OPTIONAL: Surface SendGrid delivery failures (sender unverified → 403) into audit log details rather than just app log.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 991 lines — splitting into routers (auth, clearances, admin, uploads) is overdue for maintainability.

bulk-process correctly handles 'not_found', 'already_processed', and 'others_pending:N' skip reasons; rejection emails use fire-and-forget asyncio.create_task — failures only logged, not retried (acceptable per requirement).

Cursor pagination uses ISO-8601 timestamp string comparison ($lt) which is correct for chronologically-sortable ISO timestamps; if timestamps were stored as native BSON Date this would still work but would be safer (string compare assumes UTC offset is consistent).
validate_password_strength returns the message via HTTPException detail — UI gets a clear, user-actionable error.
Faculty seeding is idempotent (only inserts when email missing) — safe across restarts.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@minsu.edu.ph / Admin@123. Faculty (all 6 offices) password: Faculty@2026. All seeded faculty verified working via login.
seed_data_creation: Per-run unique it2_student_<run>, it2_rej_<run>, it2_weak_*, it2_admweak_*, it2_strong_*, it2_reset_* users + 3 bulk-test clearances created and cleaned up in TestZCleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two complementary backend test suites now exist: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (38 iter-1 baseline, run-id-prefixed test_*) and /app/backend/tests/test_iteration2.py (32 iter-2 NEW: CORS, password complexity, faculty seeding, bulk-process, cursor pagination). CORS test hits localhost:8001 directly because the kubernetes ingress at the preview URL injects ACAO:* which masks the app's tightened CORS. SendGrid sender is unverified — emails return 403 but app handles fire-and-forget. To verify cursor pagination on a fresh DB, audit_logs grow naturally from each test run (login/register/etc emit entries). Faculty office slugs must match exactly: universitylibrarian, guidancecounselor, sasdirector_coordinator, studentaffairs_finance, collegedean_programchair, registrar.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs blocking iteration 2 functionality. The one notable observation is the ingress-layer CORS override, which is environmental and not an app bug; the app-level implementation in server.py is correct (verified via direct hit to uvicorn on port 8001).
